seoTitle: Send Microsoft Forms Responses to Excel and Email
metaDescription: Use Power Automate to log Microsoft Forms responses into Excel Online and trigger notification emails automatically, without manual exports.
intro: Manually exporting Microsoft Forms responses and copying them into a spreadsheet wastes time and creates delays before your team even knows a form was submitted. This guide shows how to build a Power Automate flow that logs every response into Excel Online and sends an email notification the moment someone submits.

steps.heading: Create the automated flow trigger
steps.body: Start a new cloud flow in Power Automate using the Microsoft Forms trigger that fires whenever a response is submitted to your target form.

steps.heading: Get the response details
steps.body: Add the action that retrieves the full response, which lets you pull each individual answer as a variable you can use later in the flow.

steps.heading: Extract the response data
steps.body: Connect your form ID and response ID so Power Automate can pull the specific submitted values, such as name, email, or any custom question fields.

steps.heading: Add a row to your Excel Online table
steps.body: Format your destination spreadsheet as a table, then use the Add a Row action to map each form field to its matching Excel column.

steps.heading: Send an email notification
steps.body: Add an Outlook 'Send an Email' action, write a subject line, and insert the dynamic form values into the email body so you get an instant alert for each submission.

steps.heading: Test and verify the flow
steps.body: Run a manual test submission and confirm the row appears correctly in Excel and the notification email arrives with the right data.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Power Automate license to do this?
faq.answer: Basic flows like this one typically work within the Power Automate license bundled with many Microsoft 365 plans, though premium connectors can require an upgraded plan.

faq.question: Can I send the notification to more than one person?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add multiple recipients in the Send an Email action or route different fields to different people based on the form data.

faq.question: What happens if the Excel file is open when the flow runs?
faq.answer: Excel Online generally handles automated row additions fine even while the file is open, but it's still good practice to avoid heavy manual editing at the same time.

faq.question: Why isn't my form data showing up in Excel?
faq.answer: This is usually a field mapping issue — double-check that each dynamic content token in the Add a Row action matches the correct form question.
